verdict: PASS — all hard checks clear. 'algorithm' is defined inline on first use in §4; WHILE/FOR EACH keywords removed from §5.4 table. Soft findings: word count (~2855w) is ~4.8% below standard corpus floor of 3000w (within 30% hard-fail tolerance); one soft readability note (rhombus used as parenthetical synonym without definition — negligible for this audience).

citation_density.detail: Citation markers [1], [2], [3] appear consistently throughout sections 5–7. Primary resource [1] (visual-paradigm.com) cited in §4, §5.1, §5.2 (Shapes 1–4), §5.6, §6, §7, §8, and §9. Supporting resources [2] and [3] cited across sections 5–7. No unresolvable citation markers found. Estimated ~65% of standalone factual assertions in §§5–7 carry citation markers, exceeding the 60% threshold.

sequence_drift.detail: 'algorithm' — previously a hard-fail forward reference — is now defined inline on first use in §4: 'an algorithm — a set of step-by-step instructions for completing a task.' All subsequent uses in §§5.1, 5.2, 5.5, and 7 are valid back-references to that definition. The WHILE/FOR EACH keyword issue is resolved: §5.4 mapping table now uses only 'Repetition' (no pseudocode-keyword specifics). All other technical terms (flowchart, terminator, process box, decision diamond, input/output parallelogram, flowline, loop) are introduced in this topic's concepts_introduced. Terms carried forward from prior topics (sequence, decision, condition, repetition, IF/OTHERWISE, pseudocode, input/process/output, abstraction) appear within their defined scope. No forward references detected.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~2855 words is ~145 words (4.8%) below the standard corpus floor of 3000 words. This is well within the 30% hard-fail tolerance. Soft finding only: the corpus could benefit from modest expansion in §5.6 (loops) and the §6 worked example to reach standard depth.

topic_scope.detail: No BPMN, swim lanes, UML activity diagrams, or data flow diagrams introduced. 'More complex process diagrams' and 'search and sort algorithms' are each named in a single sentence with an explicit 'later in the course' pointer — compliant mention-and-defer. 'infinite loop' appears only in the §8 Avoid table as a one-line caution label, not taught or defined at depth. §7 real-world patterns (troubleshooting guides, customer service scripts, software design reviews, business process documentation) stays within basic flowcharting use cases. No scope_creep findings.

findings.detail: Used as a parenthetical synonym in Shape 3's definition ('a diamond (rhombus) shape'). Not a computing term — purely geometric — and the primary label 'diamond' is clear to any beginner. Negligible impact; no action required.

beginner_readability.detail: Prose is direct and beginner-friendly throughout. 'algorithm' is now defined on first use in §4 before any technical deployment. Each of the five shapes has its own subsection with a bold definition, plain-language explanation, and inline example. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. No hedging phrases detected. Sentence lengths are consistently under 25 words. Active voice and second-person address ('you') maintained throughout.
